task: Add the next Pro backend durability gate before real hosted intent storage: keep execution preview-only, add a reusable server-side intent durability gate payload that composes execution admission, vault-handle, quota reservation, idempotency, intent-store, worker-lease, retry, and result-commit prerequisites; expose it through the Rust API and /graph web shell; update docs and guardrails evidence without adding credentials or provider execution.
securityRequirements: Do not add credential fields, provider key entry, auth token handling, provider execution, worker execution, billing mutation, or quota mutation.
The new durability gate must remain preview-only and must keep durable intent creation rejected until real hosted auth, vault, quota, lease, retry, replay, and commit stores exist.
No secrets, API keys, JWTs, bearer tokens, provider keys, vault handles, quota reservation ids, admission tokens, or durable intent ids may be emitted.
dependencyRequirements: Do not add Rust, npm, Python, image, icon, font, or external asset dependencies.
performanceRequirements: Keep the payload pure/in-memory and reuse existing Maud/fetchJson patterns.
understandingRequirements: Explain that this is a backend/interface gate for future hosted intent storage, not real durable hosted execution.
continuityRequirements: Reuse the existing ExecutionAdmissionRequest, execution_intent_create_request_preview, execution_intent_store_boundary, worker_lease_boundary, and result/preflight boundary vocabulary.
acknowledgedSkips: agent-guardrails plan inferred an unrelated generic auth scope, so this contract is manually narrowed to the Pro Rust paths.
patternSummary: Pro backend slices expose keyless preview-only Rust payloads through Axum endpoints and render them in the server-rendered Maud /graph shell. Existing payloads are denied by default and document blockers, safeguards, and next required steps.
smallestViableChange: Add a rejected ExecutionIntentDurabilityGate payload in pro/crates/queue, expose POST /api/execution-intents/durability-gate, render it on /graph, and cover it with Rust tests/docs/evidence.
assumptions: The next Pro feature should advance hosted intent-store readiness without enabling live persistence or execution.
Existing local stores remain local alpha boundaries and are not hosted tenant stores.
The UI remains a preview/debug surface for Pro alpha operators.
acceptanceCriteria: A reusable server-side durability gate payload exists and composes admission/create-request, intent-store, worker-lease, retry/idempotency, and result-commit prerequisites.
The Pro API exposes the new payload through a keyless POST endpoint.
The /graph page can request and render the gate without adding credentials or changing the dialogue-only homepage.
Tests prove the gate is rejected, preview-only, secret-free, and names the missing hosted prerequisites.
Docs/evidence describe the new boundary and remaining blockers.
nonGoals: No credential collection or vault access
No quota reservation or billing mutation
No worker lease claims or retries
expectedBehaviorChanges: Pro API gains a preview-only intent durability gate endpoint.
Pro /graph gains a panel/button to inspect the gate before hosted intent storage exists.
nfrRequirements: Keep readable UI copy, no remote assets, no secret-shaped values, and no broad refactors.
expectedConcurrencyImpact: None. The new gate is a pure preview payload and does not mutate stores.
observabilityRequirements: The payload must make blocking prerequisites inspectable through explicit lists.
rollbackNotes: Revert this branch commit; it adds no migrations, dependencies, hosted stores, or runtime secrets.
